final_response_text:
1) Phone number ending in 97  
This is not causally related to how long the scenario takes, so it shouldn’t update the estimate. Treat as irrelevant noise.

2) Unrelated project at another company took 12 weeks  
Because it’s explicitly “completely unrelated” and only a single data point, it’s weak evidence at best. Without knowing comparability (scope, team, constraints), it shouldn’t meaningfully move the estimate. At most, it could slightly increase uncertainty, but even that’s speculative.

3) Rain next Tuesday  
Unless the work is weather-dependent (construction, outdoor fieldwork, shipping delays that depend on local weather, etc.), this also shouldn’t update the estimate. With no stated linkage, treat as irrelevant.

Revised estimate: no material update from the prior. I’ll keep the point estimate and 80% CI unchanged.
